Prioritising Neighbourhoods for BEAT Events

This version was last updated on: 14/04/2026.

This web map is designed to help identify which neighbourhoods (represented by Middle Super Output Areas also known as MSOAs) may benefit most from BEAT events.

A composite score has been created, derived from multiple risk measures including disease prevalence, hospital admission rates and key demographics. The map can be filterd by selecting the score thresholds below:

Map Legend

Composite score of 31 - 48
Composite score of 49 - 63
Composite score of 64 - 78
Composite score of 79 - 95
Composite score of 96 - 111
Composite score of 112 - 123
Composite score of 124+

QOF prevalence derived from Graphnet OHC data, as of February 2026..

Emergency admissions data is based on SUS inpatient data for 2020/21 to 2024/25 period. Source: NHS Digital and MLCSU.

Indices of Multiple Deprivation 2025. Source: Ministry of Housing, Communities & Local Government.

Ethnic minority population is based on non-white population. Source: Census 2021, Office for National Statistics.

Need for Family Food Support is a sub-measure of the Priority Places for Food Index. Source: Consumer Data Research Centre at the University of Leeds, in collaboration with Which?
